title: Tithymalus correllii
taxonomy: Tithymalus correllii is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family. It belongs to the order Malpighiales, which is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the tropics and subtropics. At the genus level, it is part of the monotypic genus Tithymalus, which comprises a small number of species. Tithymalus correllii is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, where it grows in dry areas. It is a succulent perennial that produces small, white flowers.

intro: Tithymalus correllii, also known as the prickly milkweed or correll's sandmat, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Tithymalus correllii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tithymalus correllii is often prepared as a tincture or salve, and is used to treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
